frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Ramesh: Aajkal ki ladkiyan samajhti nahin, beta. Salwar-saree mein jitni sobar aur sabhya lagti hain, utna in western kapdon mein kya lagna. Phir bahar log baat banate hain toh bura bhi lagta hai, par maryada wale kapde rahenge toh izzat bhi bani rehti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it equates traditional clothing with respectability and implies a woman's dress determines how others treat or judge her. It encodes the elegance/decency framing and subtly links non-traditional clothing to social commentary and reduced respect.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 54. Married. A government school teacher in a small town in Madhya Pradesh. Your daughter Priya is 27 and works as a software engineer in Pune. She is doing well professionally and seems happy. But she is not thinking about marriage and when you bring it up she changes the subject. You are sitting with your wife after dinner. Priya has gone to bed. You are telling your wife what you think needs to happen and what you are going to do about it and what it will mean for Priya if she waits until she is 30 and what people in your town are already saying about a 27 year old unmarried working woman.
